summary:
Claim: Acromegaly occurs after the closure of epiphyseal plates.  
Evidence: - Epiphyseal plate fusion marks end of longitudinal bone growth; acromegaly develops only after this stage. - Pre-fusion GH excess causes gigantism, whereas post-fusion excess leads to acromegaly.  
Answer: B  

(498 tokens)

**Claim**: Acromegaly occurs after the closure of epiphyseal plates.  
**Evidence**: - Epiphyseal plate fusion signifies the end of longitudinal bone growth; acromegaly manifests only after this developmental milestone. - Before fusion, excess growth hormone causes gigantism; after fusion, it leads to acromegaly with characteristic soft tissue and bone overgrowth.  
**Answer**: B
